up.units: not reported as absolute rate (nitrite oxidation activity expressed only as relative % / time-to-deplete)

sec.units: not quantified as rate; nitrate >15 mM self-inhibitory to Nitrospira

growth_records.cite: Murakami, Chiho et al. Mutualistic relationship between Nitrospira and concomitant heterotrophs. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2022). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.13030. [PMC9300095]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rate (mu) and lag time (lambda) of Nitrospira among cultivation with IN-medium, 10% NSM-medium and 10% HSM-media ... The data are expressed as relative values with that in IN-medium defined as 1.0.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (nitrite-oxidizing bacterium Nitrospira sp. ND1) but NO absolute experimental rates reported: specific growth rate mu and lag time lambda are given only as RELATIVE values (IN-medium = 1.0) in Fig 2C and qualitatively (+/±/-) in Table 1; nitrite oxidation activity reported as relative % (Fig 2B). No absolute mu, uptake or secretion rate is extractable from the main text (methods/quantities in Appendix S1, not present here). Only quantitative datum: spent Nitrospira medium (NSM) contained 15.9 +/- 6.3 mg-C/L dissolved organic carbon; nitrate inhibitory above 15 mM. Record retained to document the prokaryote condition; rate fields null by design (not invented). Co-culture partners incl. Pseudomonas putida NBRC14164, Mycobacterium spp., Shinella fusca, Ensifer adhaerens etc.
